highlights.description: Canonical event model and Collector contract for developer-experience telemetry (OmniDevX domain)
Local JSONL event store: idempotent, inspectable, reprocessable
Three collectors: Claude Code session history, git commits with AI co-author attribution, and a live OTLP/JSON metrics receiver
Metadata-only privacy model enforced structurally and by tests: no prompt text, responses, or file contents are ever captured

added.description: Canonical `Event` IR (omnidevx.event/v1): deterministic IDs, ai./devx. event-type namespaces, shared attribute keys, provenance with collection mode and confidence
`Collector` interface with `CollectRequest`/`CollectionResult`, half-open `Period` semantics, and diagnostics instead of silent drops
`store`: daily JSONL files per source under ~/.plexusone/omnidevx/data with owner-only permissions, ID-deduplicated idempotent writes, period/product-scoped reads, and damaged-line recovery

`providers/claudecode`: stdlib-only importer for ~/.claude/projects session JSONL — prompts (sidechains excluded), assistant messages with model and token usage, tool completions via tool_use/tool_result correlation, session spans

`providers/git`: devx.change.committed events built on github.com/grokify/gogit, with change stats, author identity, commit-hash IDs that deduplicate clones, and AI attribution from Co-authored-by trailers via the canonical KnownAITools registry

`providers/genericotel`: OTLP/HTTP (JSON) receiver converting Claude Code and Codex CLI token/cost metrics to ai.usage.recorded events with otel-mode provenance; log payloads acknowledged and discarded

tests.description: Contract, store, and provider tests on fabricated fixtures only: period boundaries, JSON round-trips, store idempotency and damaged-line recovery, per-provider parsing, OTLP ingestion idempotency, and privacy assertions that content-like attribute keys fail the build

documentation.description: README with OmniDXI disambiguation and usage
MkDocs site: canonical events, event store, privacy model, and per-provider guides including Claude Code OTel setup

infrastructure.description: CI workflows (go-ci, go-lint, go-sast-codeql), golangci-lint config, dependabot, MIT license, gitignore
